hero_eyebrow: Open Source · Self-Hosted · Privacy-First · Local by Default
hero_h1:
Every agent. One memory.
Total recall.
hero_lead:
A self-hosted personal memory layer for AI agents — built on SQLite or Postgres, on-device embeddings and MCP.
Runs 100% local by default. No API keys. No cloud dependency. Your data never leaves your machine.
You are a software developer? ✦ Try Aperio-dev — basic terminal comfort is required.
You are a normal human? ✦ Try Aperio-lite — double-click and go, no tech skills needed.
terminal_semantic_ready: pgvector enabled — semantic search active (16/16 memories embedded)
terminal_port_story: — the original l33t port. Famous in the 90s, introduced by the group 'Cult of the Dead Cow' in 1998, a ghost town on modern machines today. Ours now. Too iconic not to use! He-he ;/
features_lead: Built for developers who want AI that actually knows them. Without giving up their data or their infrastructure.
feat_memory_desc: Your memories survive every conversation, every tool, every session. 8 structured types keep things organized — facts, preferences, decisions, solutions and more.
feat_self_memory_desc: The AI keeps its own private notebook — walled off from your memories. It learns what works, remembers your style, and carries its own observations across every session.
feat_search_desc: Powered by on-device embeddings (mxbai-embed-large) over sqlite-vec or pgvector. Ask about your projects and get results matched by meaning, not keywords. Voyage AI optional.
feat_mcp_desc: One brain shared across every agent. Claude, Cursor, Windsurf — all connect to the same Postgres database through the MCP protocol.
feat_stream_desc: Responses stream token by token via WebSocket. Live code rendering, markdown on completion, smart auto-scroll. No waiting.
feat_local_desc: Runs fully on your machine with Ollama — free, private, offline-capable. Switch to Claude with one env variable when you need more power.
feat_dedup_desc: Background job finds near-duplicate memories every 10 minutes using cosine similarity. Dry-run by default — you stay in control.
feat_reason_desc: Native support for thinking models like qwen3, gemma4 and llama3 — and cloud models reason out of the box. A collapsible reasoning bubble shows the model's thought process live. Toggle it on or off anytime.
feat_roundtable_desc: Toggle Discuss and two agents cross-review each other until they agree. Domain characters give each a different perspective.
feat_database_desc: Connect SQLite, Postgres or MySQL and ask in plain English. Aperio writes the SQL — reads run instantly, and any change to your data asks you to confirm first.
feat_team_desc: Since you own the database, Aperio scales from personal to shared team brain with two changes — update the system prompt and seed team memories. One brain for the whole team.
arch_lead: One database. One MCP server. Two AI providers. Your brain is just a Postgres table with vectors.
tools_lead: Each one is available to any MCP-compatible agent (Claude, Cursor, Windsurf).
tool_remember_desc: Save a new memory — auto-generates embedding for semantic retrieval
tool_recall_desc: Semantic search with cosine similarity, FTS fallback
tool_update_memory_desc: Edit by UUID — new version, old tombstoned
tool_forget_desc: Delete a specific memory by UUID
tool_self_remember_desc: Agent saves a note to its own private store — walled off from user memories
tool_self_recall_desc: Agent searches its own notes semantically or by full-text
tool_self_update_desc: Agent revises one of its own notes in-place
tool_self_forget_desc: Agent deletes one of its own notes
tool_read_file_desc: Read any file from disk (max 500 lines)
tool_append_file_desc: Append content to end of file
tool_read_docx_desc: Read and extract text from .docx files
tool_scan_project_desc: Scan folder tree up to 3 levels deep
tool_wiki_write_desc: Create or update a cited wiki article from memories
tool_wiki_search_desc: Hybrid FTS + semantic search across wiki articles
tool_wiki_get_desc: Fetch a wiki article by slug with revision info
tool_code_search_desc: Search indexed code symbols by name or doc text
tool_code_repos_desc: List all indexed repos with symbol counts
tool_code_outline_desc: List every symbol in a file with line ranges
tool_code_context_desc: Fetch source slice for a symbol by qualified name
tool_code_callers_desc: Find symbols that call the given target
tool_code_callees_desc: Find symbols called by the given target
tool_doc_search_desc: Search indexed document passages by meaning
tool_doc_repos_desc: List all indexed doc folders with chunk counts
tool_doc_context_desc: Fetch text of one section or chunk
tool_run_node_script_desc: Execute Node.js scripts in a sandbox
tool_run_python_script_desc: Execute Python scripts in a sandbox
tool_syntax_check_desc: Validate code syntax for supported languages
tool_run_shell_desc: Execute shell commands with output capture
tool_fetch_url_desc: Fetch a URL, strip HTML, truncate to 15k chars
tool_web_search_desc: Search the web via DuckDuckGo, return ranked results
tool_read_image_desc: Read and OCR-extract text from images
tool_preprocess_image_desc: Optimize image for local vision models
tool_describe_image_desc: Generate a text description of an image
tool_fetch_github_issue_desc: Read a GitHub issue with comments and metadata
tool_create_github_issue_desc: Open a new issue on a GitHub repository
tool_list_github_issues_desc: List open issues for triage across repos
tool_record_issue_triage_desc: Record a triage verdict for an issue
tool_export_data_desc: Export memories & wiki to a portable JSON file
tool_import_data_desc: Import memories & wiki from an export file
tool_db_connections_desc: List available database connections (never exposes credentials)
tool_db_schema_desc: Introspect tables, columns, indexes, and foreign keys
tool_db_query_desc: Run ONE read-only SQL statement with parameterized bindings
tool_db_execute_desc: Propose a write/DDL statement — confirm-before-write flow
build_lead:
Aperio is a foundation, not a finished product. The source is fully open — fork it, extend it, repurpose it.
Here's what you can build on top.
build_mem_desc: The schema is yours to extend. Add new memory types, extra metadata columns, TTL logic, or per-project namespacing. Postgres gives you full flexibility.
build_mcp_desc: Adding a tool is just a new entry in mcp/index.js. Expose calendar access, email drafting, browser control — any action you want your agents to take.
build_ai_desc: mxbai-embed-large runs on-device via transformers by default — zero external calls. Swap in Voyage AI for higher quality, or drop in OpenAI, Cohere, or any provider with a vectors API.
build_ui_desc: The web interface is a standalone HTML file with WebSocket. Rip it out and build a VS Code extension, a mobile app, a CLI, a Chrome sidebar — the server API stays the same.
build_data_desc: Query your own memory graph. Build dashboards showing what topics you think about most, decision patterns over time, knowledge gaps, or memory growth by week.
build_team_desc: Since you own the database, Aperio scales from personal to team brain with two changes: update the system prompt to team context, seed team memories in 001_init.sql.
build_agent_desc: Run multiple specialized agents that all share one brain. A research agent stores findings, a coding agent reads context, a writing agent pulls preferences — all from the same database.
build_background_desc: Scheduled, autonomous agents that run without a chat turn. Define jobs in the database, set interval or file-watcher triggers, and let them handle deduplication, wiki refresh, code indexing — silently, in the background.
build_cta_title: Got an idea? Build it and share it.
build_cta_sub: Open a PR, open an issue, or fork it and take it somewhere new. Aperio is a starting point.
compare_lead:
Commercial memory services are great products.
Aperio is a different choice — self-hosted, open source, fully customizable.
compare_ap_team: Shared DB — one brain for the team
webui_lead: Local AI · persistent memory · streaming responses · 4 themes.
setup_lead: Three ways in — pick your comfort level. Everything runs 100% local by default: no API keys, no cloud.
setup_method_lite_desc: No code, no terminal. Download → unzip → double-click. A browser wizard installs everything and picks a model that fits your machine.
setup_comment_clone_release: # clones the release branch → starts Aperio
setup_comment_rerun: # re-run anytime to update in place —
setup_method_source_desc: Full control — clone the dev branch and run it yourself. Node 18+; SQLite by default, Postgres optional. Step-by-step below.
setup_comment_dev_branch: # dedicated developer branch - no extra files
step2_title: Ready for use .env.example in the project folder
setup_comment_database_url: # DATABASE_URL=postgresql://…  — only if you choose Postgres
setup_comment_sqlite: # default: SQLite + sqlite-vec — nothing to install
setup_comment_start_engine: # start the engine from separate terminal
setup_comment_embeddings: # embeddings run on-device (transformers) — no model to pull
step6_title: Use Aperio chat in the terminal
setup_comment_docker_detect: # auto-detect if any Docker - uses SQLite as fallback
setup_tip: ✅ Tip: Check out project README.md for extensive documentation, troubleshooting, and advanced configuration options.
lite_h2:
Small tool. Big ideas. 🧐
No coding skills required.
lite_lead:
The fastest path to your own private AI.
Runs 100% on your machine. No Money. No Cloud. No register your email here and there.
Download → unzip → double-click. That's it.
lite_flow_header: the whole flow — once is enough
lite_macos_steps:
1. Download, unzip, open in Finder
2. START.sh is right at the top level (the how-to folder holds a printable guide)
3. Find START.sh → right-click → Make Alias
4. Rename the alias to Aperio
5.A Drag alias to Desktop folder in Finder's sidebar
❗ Sidebar item — not the Desktop surface itself
5.B Hold ⌥ Option + ⌘ Cmd and drag START.sh directly onto the desktop → rename it there
6. Double-click → one-time setup → done
✅ Default browser opens automatically at
http://localhost:31337
lite_windows_steps:
1. Download, unzip, open in File Explorer
2. START.bat is right at the top level (the how-to folder holds a printable guide)
3. Find START.bat → right-click → Rename
4. Rename the file to Aperio.bat
5.A Move shortcut to Desktop
5.B Hold Alt and drag START.bat directly onto the desktop → rename it there
6. Double-click → one-time setup → done
✅ Default browser opens automatically at
http://localhost:31337
lite_linux_steps:
1. Download, unzip, open in your file manager
2. START.sh is right at the top level (the how-to folder holds a printable guide)
3. Find START.sh → right-click → Properties → Permissions
4. Enable Allow executing file as program → close
5. Right-click START.sh → Copy to Desktop → rename to Aperio
❗ Menu labels vary by distro (Nautilus, Dolphin, etc.)
6. Double-click → one-time setup → done
✅ Default browser opens automatically at
http://localhost:31337
lite_no_skills_desc:
Nothing to configure.
✅ No databases to set up.
✅ No config files to edit.
✅ No environment variables.
✅ No package managers.
Just download and double-click.
First run installs everything automatically in the background.
You'll see a terminal window — that's normal.
The terminal is yours GOD EYE on what is happening.
lite_updates_desc:
Download the latest release.
✅ Windows
- Right-click on the zip and select "Extract All..."
- In the dialog, specify your aperio-lite folder.
✅ macOS/Linux (Terminal - fastest)
- unzip aperio-lite.zip -d ~/aperio-lite
Installed with the one-liner? Even easier.
Re-run the install command (or git pull in the folder) — it fast-forwards to the latest release and keeps your memory database. No re-download.
Your data stays untouched.
❗ Might want to export your memories first.
- You'll have your data even if the update fails
lite_uninstall_desc:
Every run after the first run?
Double-click the desktop shortcut. That's it.
Terminal: bash START.sh
✅ If you can open a browser, you can run Aperio.
How to remove it if I do not like it?
1. Delete the Desktop alias / shortcut
2. Delete the unzipped folder
✅ That's it. Completely gone.
- No registry entries. No hidden files.
- No traces. Nothing.
cta_tagline: Open source · local by default · free forever
cta_h2:
Your AI has been
amnesiac?
Fix that today.
cta_origin: From Latin aperire — to open, to reveal, to bring into the light ✨
footer_warning: ⚠️ Warning: Excessive use of AI agents may cause your brain to atrophy, leading to irreversible stupidity. Use responsibly.
